Politics.headline: House GOP leaders plan housing bill vote despite Trump ultimatum
Politics.detail: House Republican leaders are moving forward with a vote on a housing bill, despite President Trump's opposition and ultimatum.

Politics.headline: Treasury extends Russian oil sanctions waiver for another month
Politics.detail: The U.S. Treasury Department has extended a waiver on sanctions related to Russian oil for another month, indicating continued pressure on oil markets.

Politics.headline: Democratic district attorneys vow to prosecute federal agents who target voting sites
Politics.detail: Democratic district attorneys are pledging to prosecute federal agents who target voting sites, raising concerns about potential conflicts during elections.

Politics.headline: US drops fraud charges after billionaire Adani pledges $10bn investment
Politics.detail: The U.S. government has dropped fraud charges against billionaire Adani after he pledged a $10 billion investment, following accusations of bribing Indian officials.

Politics.headline: Trump DOJ creates $1.7-billion fund for victims of legal 'weaponization,' prompting outrage
Politics.detail: The Trump administration's Justice Department is creating a $1.7-billion fund for victims of legal 'weaponization,' sparking outrage and concerns over misuse of funds.

Finance & Economy.headline: Standard Chartered to cut more than 7,000 jobs as it steps up AI use
Finance & Economy.detail: Standard Chartered is planning to reduce its workforce by over 7,000 positions as it increases its adoption of artificial intelligence technologies. The move reflects a broader trend in the financial industry to streamline operations and leverage AI for enhanced efficiency.

Finance & Economy.headline: UK unemployment unexpectedly rises to 5% as firms squeezed by Iran war
Finance & Economy.detail: The UK unemployment rate has unexpectedly increased to 5% as companies face pressure from the ongoing Iran war. This rise indicates a potential slowdown in the UK economy as businesses react to geopolitical tensions.

Finance & Economy.headline: NextEra to buy Dominion in $67bn deal creating US utility giant
Finance & Economy.detail: NextEra is set to acquire Dominion in a $67 billion deal, which will establish a major utility company in the United States. This acquisition reflects the increasing demand for energy, driven in part by the expansion of AI technologies.

Finance & Economy.headline: Elon Musk loses US lawsuit against OpenAI
Finance & Economy.detail: Elon Musk has lost his lawsuit against OpenAI. The details of the suit are not mentioned in the snippet.

Finance & Economy.headline: Eurozone Trade Surplus Plunges in March as Exports Slump
Finance & Economy.detail: The Eurozone's trade surplus dramatically decreased to €7.8 billion in March 2026, a significant drop from €34.1 billion the previous year, due to a 5.5% fall in exports to €265.3 billion. This decline is attributed to decreased shipments in chemicals, machinery, vehicles, and food and drink, with a notable 38.8% plunge in exports to the US.

Technology.headline: Google I/O 2026: Live updates as the company announces new features for Gemini, AI, Search and more
Technology.detail: Join the liveblog for real-time news from Google's developer conference, covering announcements about Gemini, AI, Search and other features.

Technology.headline: Meta is reportedly 'reassigning' 7,000 employees to AI-focused roles
Technology.detail: The company is reportedly transferring 7,000 employees to four new organizations that are focused on building AI tools and apps.

Technology.headline: The jury in the OpenAI case has ruled against Elon Musk
Technology.detail: After a three-week trial, the jury deliberated for less than two hours before ruling against Elon Musk in the OpenAI case.

Technology.headline: Google I/O 2026: Everything We Expect and How to Watch
Technology.detail: Google's annual developer conference is happening today, May 19, and is expected to feature significant updates to Gemini and Android Auto, along with more information on Googlebooks powered by Aluminum OS.

Technology.headline: The Original Chromebook Dream Is Dead. Googlebooks Killed It.
Technology.detail: Commentary discusses how Googlebooks are abandoning the original idea of Chromebooks as efficient, web-based computing in favor of embracing AI and Android apps.

Science.headline: SMILE spacecraft launches to capture first X-ray views of Earth's magnetic shield
Science.detail: The Smile spacecraft, a collaboration between ESA and CAS, lifted off on a Vega-C rocket to study how Earth responds to solar activity, using X-ray and ultraviolet cameras to observe Earth's magnetic shield and the northern lights. The mission aims to improve models of Earth's magnetic environment and protect space technologies.

Science.headline: Molecular net boosts the power of natural biopesticides
Science.detail: Scientists have discovered that Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) uses a 'sporesilk' network of protein fibers to trap spores and toxins, enhancing its ability to kill insect pests. This nanofiber net, stable under extreme conditions, helps keep spores and toxins together for effective infection.

Science.headline: String theory suddenly emerged from simple physics rules
Science.detail: Physicists have found that the equations describing particle behavior at extreme energies naturally produce fingerprints of string theory, suggesting the universe may favor its existence. Researchers started with basic rules about particle interactions and discovered that string theory's characteristics emerged without assuming strings from the outset.

Science.headline: Study Finds Potential Dark 'Matter Imprint' In Gravitational Waves
Science.detail: A new model predicts how dark matter could subtly distort gravitational waves produced during black hole mergers, potentially revealing clues about dark matter. When tested on real LIGO data, one signal stood out as potentially carrying a dark matter imprint.

Science.headline: Scientists boosted one protein and aging mice became stronger and healthier
Science.detail: By boosting levels of a specific protein, scientists made older mice stronger, more energetic, and gave them healthier bones. The protein appears to inhibit chronic inflammation linked to aging, potentially leading to therapies for healthier aging in humans.

Health.headline: Congo health ministry reports 131 deaths and 513 suspected Ebola cases in eastern Congo
Health.detail: At least 131 deaths and over 500 suspected cases have been reported in the ongoing Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo, caused by the rare Bundibugyo virus, for which there are no approved therapeutics or vaccines; the WHO has declared the outbreak a public health emergency of international concern.

Health.headline: Steep drop in number of people with Affordable Care Act health coverage, analysis finds
Health.detail: A new analysis projects that enrollment in Affordable Care Act marketplaces will plunge this year, potentially leaving millions uninsured due to the expiration of enhanced premium tax credits. Costs went up significantly and a lot of people dropped their plans.

Health.headline: The Global Fund and Africa CDC Join Forces for Stronger, Self-Reliant Health Systems
Health.detail: The Global Fund is intensifying its collaboration with regional partners to drive impact and support countries on their path toward self-reliance. A new Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) aims to accelerate progress toward ending AIDS, tuberculosis and malaria by 2030 while contributing to stronger, sustainable and more self-reliant health systems across the continent.

Health.headline: Here's what doctors say would happen if measles spread hit during the FIFA World Cup
Health.detail: Canada's health agency is warning of a “high risk” of measles spread from the domestic and international tourists that will be mingling for the matches in Toronto and Vancouver for the FIFA World Cup, which could overwhelm the already overburdened health-care system.

Health.headline: American doctor working in Congo tests positive for Ebola
Health.detail: An American medical missionary in the Democratic Republic of the Congo has a confirmed case of Ebola, and is being taken to Germany for treatment.

Sports.headline: Aaron Rai runs away with the PGA Championship, first English-born winner in more than a century
Sports.detail: Aaron Rai secured a dominant victory at the PGA Championship, becoming the first English-born player to win the tournament in over a century.

Sports.headline: Neymar picked for Brazil's World Cup squad despite doubts on fitness
Sports.detail: Despite concerns about his fitness, Neymar has been selected for Brazil's World Cup squad.

Sports.headline: Arsenal on brink of Premier League title with Man City needing to beat Bournemouth.
Sports.detail: Arsenal is close to winning the Premier League title, but Manchester City needs to win against Bournemouth to keep their hopes alive.

Sports.headline: Aaron Rodgers agrees to a 1-year deal to return to the Pittsburgh Steelers, AP sources say.
Sports.detail: According to AP sources, Aaron Rodgers has agreed to a one-year deal to return to the Pittsburgh Steelers.

Sports.headline: Pep Guardiola Set To Depart Manchester City; Enzo Maresca Expected To Take Over
Sports.detail: Pep Guardiola is set to leave Manchester City, with Enzo Maresca expected to take over as manager.

Entertainment.headline: Boy band Ateez to headline BST Hyde Park music event
Entertainment.detail: K-pop group Ateez will headline the British Summer Time Hyde Park concert series on June 28, marking another significant global festival appearance for the group. The annual event, held in London's Hyde Park, has previously featured headliners such as Taylor Swift and Adele.

Entertainment.headline: Girl group aespa to refresh Seoul with citywide pop-ups for 'Lemonade' release
Entertainment.detail: K-pop girl group aespa is set to launch pop-up events across Seoul and overseas to celebrate their second full-length album, 'Lemonade', starting May 29. The pop-ups, titled 'aespa WEEK — Make It Lemonade,' will include outdoor riverside locations and a themed space at IFC Mall, along with merchandise and photo booths.

Entertainment.headline: Boy band xikers marks explosive start to its newest era with EP 'Route Zero: The Ora'
Entertainment.detail: The boy band xikers has launched its newest era with the EP 'Route Zero: The Ora,' featuring the lead track “OKay,” which they presented at a press showcase in Seoul. The EP features five songs and is marked by high-impact live performances and explosive choreography.

Entertainment.headline: Celebrity Treasure Island competitor on why he didn't use advantage card
Entertainment.detail: TVNZ sports reporter Zion Dayal explained his decision not to use his advantage card on Celebrity Treasure Island after being eliminated in a balancing challenge. Dayal, who described himself as feeling out of place among the celebrity cast, had knocked out some of the competition's biggest names prior to his elimination.

Entertainment.headline: METRO CHANNEL is premiering three new food and travel shows this May
Entertainment.detail: METRO CHANNEL is premiering three new food and travel shows this May, serving up stories of cheesemaking, culinary adventures, and new travel experiences. The shows include Cheese: Searching for a Taste of Place, Jeremy Pang's Hong Kong Kitchen, and Brent Owens: Extreme, Authentic & Unwrapped, airing on Sundays via Metro Channel.

Crime & Justice.headline: Eurojust supports arrest of man suspected of intentional homicide of his partner
Crime & Justice.detail: Swiss and Spanish authorities, with the support of Eurojust, arrested a man suspected of intentional homicide after his partner disappeared from Switzerland; her remains were later found on his property. The investigation into the exact cause of death continues.

Crime & Justice.headline: Three killed, two suspects dead in shooting at San Diego mosque
Crime & Justice.detail: A shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego resulted in the deaths of three people and two suspects. Police are investigating the incident as a possible hate crime.

Crime & Justice.headline: Clallam County, city Port Angeles nearing criminal justice agreements
Crime & Justice.detail: Clallam County and the city of Port Angeles are making progress in negotiations regarding criminal justice contracts, breaking them up into separate agreements for prosecution, public defense, jail, and court services. The existing contract was extended to June 30 while new terms are negotiated.

Crime & Justice.headline: N.J. State Police detective found guilty of endangering motorcyclist who died in 2023 crash
Crime & Justice.detail: Mark Campagna, a N.J. State Police detective, was found guilty of endangering Omar Kebbabi, a motorcyclist who died in a 2023 crash. Campagna was chasing Kebbabi in an unmarked vehicle without lights or sirens while off duty.

Crime & Justice.headline: Delco man charged with fatal ambush of woman killed in a West Philly gang feud she had nothing to do with, police say.
Crime & Justice.detail: Hamza Ruley, a 24 year old man from Delco, was arrested on Thursday for his alleged role in the killing of Imani Ringgold last month. Ringgold was killed in a West Philly gang feud that she had nothing to do with.

Education.headline: Google Announces New Training Programme for Teachers Across India
Education.detail: Google has announced a new training program for teachers across India, aiming to integrate AI into education.

Education.headline: NEET Re-Exam: Minister Reviews Preparations, Stresses Foolproof Conduct
Education.detail: The Education Minister reviewed preparations for the NEET-UG 2026 re-exam scheduled for June 21st, emphasizing the need for a foolproof and fair conduct of the examination.

Education.headline: Inside CBSE's On-Screen Marking System: Board Answers Students' Questions
Education.detail: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has addressed students' queries regarding the on-screen marking system used for evaluating exam papers.

Education.headline: Ryan Bodensteiner announced as new director of the Center for Evaluation and Educational Leadership
Education.detail: Ryan Bodensteiner has been appointed as the director of the Center for Evaluation and Educational Leadership (CEEL) at the University of Kansas, where he will support school district leaders in creating effective learning environments and will work closely with schools and districts to evaluate needs and develop personalized support plans.

Education.headline: Minister Manamela collapses Parliament's NSFAS administration oversight
Education.detail: Minister Buti Manamela has delayed briefing Parliament on placing NSFAS under administration, due to the Department of Higher Education and Training (DHET) and NSFAS failing to submit presentations. The DA has called for the scrapping of NSFAS in its current form and replacing it with a decentralised student funding model.

Lifestyle.headline: Bride Says She Was "Scammed" By Designer Behind Influencer's Viral Cannes Gown
Lifestyle.detail: A bride claims she was scammed by the designer of an influencer's viral gown from Cannes, alleging issues with her own dress design and construction.

Lifestyle.headline: Inside Iran's 134-Year-Old Vishnu Temple Built By Indians, With Buddha Statues
Lifestyle.detail: A 134-year-old Vishnu temple in Iran, built by Indians, houses Buddha statues, showcasing a unique blend of cultures and religious influences.

Lifestyle.headline: Pinoy educator awarded prestigious MBE in UK
Lifestyle.detail: A Filipino educator has been awarded the prestigious Member of the Order of the British Empire (MBE) in the UK for their contributions.

Lifestyle.headline: Feeling foggy and tired? Your gut health may be affecting your brain.
Lifestyle.detail: Scientists are discovering more about the connection between the brain and the gastrointestinal system, suggesting that gut health can influence mental clarity and energy levels.

Lifestyle.headline: The lifestyle changes that could add an extra five years to your life.
Lifestyle.detail: Adopting a healthy lifestyle could potentially prolong a person's life by up to five years, according to new research.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion: Colorado's caucus chaos must go, and we need a practical primary system
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Colorado's caucus system threatens the integrity and effectiveness of democracy, locking out independent voters and favoring extreme elements; it's time for a practical primary system that gives all voters a say. The current system demands a massive toll in time, money and commitment from only the most extreme elements.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Editorial: An economic whirlpool
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Rising gas prices, driven by Iran's control of the Strait of Hormuz, are pushing inflation higher than pay raises, causing consumer confidence to plummet to historic lows and jeopardizing manufacturing jobs in Ohio. With 70 percent of citizens considering the economy poor, there's less inclination to spend on items Ohio makes, which means Ohio employers should plan for a recession.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: WHA's rejection of Taiwan proposal justified: China Daily editorial
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The World Health Assembly's decision to reject Taiwan's participation for the 10th consecutive year upholds the one-China principle, demonstrating the international community's recognition of China's sovereignty over the island. The rejection underscores that Taiwan is part of China, and any attempts by the Democratic Progressive Party to push for "Taiwan independence" will lead to international isolation.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion: Why CT's many fixes don't add up
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Connecticut's legislative session is celebrated as a political success with electric bill relief and increased aid, but it doesn't address the underlying systemic issues causing the problems. The state treats interconnected problems like housing, education, and healthcare in isolation, resulting in governance that succeeds politically without structural change.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion | Undecided: To go or not to go (out)
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The author discusses their severe fear of missing out (FOMO) and the anxiety it causes when deciding whether to go out, highlighting the exhaustion of coordinating plans and the desire to appeal to others. They combat this by employing digital detoxes to tune out others' lives and focus on their own, addressing the innate need to belong that drives FOMO.

World.headline: WHO to hold emergency committee meeting as Ebola death toll rises to 131
World.detail: The World Health Organization (WHO) is convening an emergency committee meeting as the Ebola death toll climbs to 131.

World.headline: At least 10 dead as huge floods sweep southern and central China
World.detail: Severe flooding has struck southern and central China, resulting in at least 10 fatalities.

World.headline: Gunmen abduct 39 students, 7 teachers in attacks on Nigeria schools
World.detail: In Nigeria, gunmen have abducted 39 students and 7 teachers during attacks on schools.

World.headline: Spain's former PM Zapatero faces corruption probe
World.detail: Spain's former Prime Minister Zapatero is under investigation for corruption.

World.headline: Trump claims planned attack on Iran postponed after Tehran makes new proposal to end war
World.detail: Donald Trump claims a planned attack on Iran was postponed following a new proposal from Tehran to end the war.

Zimbabwe.headline: Constitution Amendment Bill No.3 public hearings went smoothly – Mnangagwa says despite violence claims
Zimbabwe.detail: President Mnangagwa stated that the public hearings regarding Constitution Amendment Bill No. 3 proceeded without issues, despite reports of violence during the process.

Zimbabwe.headline: Chivayo's ex-wife arrested over alleged cellphone theft, child access dispute
Zimbabwe.detail: Mary Taruvinga reports the arrest of Wicknell Chivayo's ex-wife in connection to allegations of cellphone theft and a dispute over child access.

Zimbabwe.headline: CAB3 extends terms by two years. Here is what that does to your vote.
Zimbabwe.detail: Valentine Maya discusses the implications of Constitution Amendment Bill (CAB) 3, which extends terms by two years, and its impact on voting rights.

Zimbabwe.headline: Harare hosts national summit on climate-smart agriculture
Zimbabwe.detail: Tatenda Kunaka reports on the national summit held in Harare focusing on climate-smart agriculture practices.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mnangagwa quietly leaves Zimbabwe for Belarus amid constitutional amendment tensions
Zimbabwe.detail: Emmerson Mnangagwa reportedly departed for Belarus, missing a judiciary event, amidst rising political tensions regarding proposed constitutional amendments that could extend his presidency.
